summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a tax budget public hearing for fiscal year 2027, a presentation from M/I Homes on a proposed residential development of 547-565 lots, and several routine administrative items. The board discussed strong financial performance including $245,000 in back-pay cannabis dispensary revenue and growing interest income, adopted an updated official zoning map, and accepted personnel changes in fire and police departments.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (April 15, 2026 through May 26, 2026) – passed
Motion to accept donation of Goshen 250 flag from CPAA – passed
Motion to accept full-time resignation of firefighter paramedic Alissa Malone Ladd and continue her as part-time – passed
Motion to authorize sale of surplus equipment (copy machine, desk, two desktop patches) on GovDeals – passed
Motion to adopt Resolution R022-2020 updating the official zoning map – passed
Motion to accept donation of $300 in food gift cards from CPAA – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Officer Gary Rausch effective May 15, 2026 – passed
Motion to adopt Resolution R021-2026 adopting the 2027 tax budget – passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:02 PM – passed
action_items: Brent Wilkins (M/I Homes) to email development plans to Director Langenheim for distribution to all three trustees
M/I Homes to explore a 'Plan C' that incorporates varied lot types but meets current PD zoning standards
Trustees to funnel feedback on M/I Homes plans through Director Langenheim
M/I Homes to bring architectural elevations and product type examples at future formal presentation
Donna Rossick to coordinate framing of Goshen 250 flag for community room wall
Administrator Rose to look into whether liquor/cigarette revenue has declined since cannabis dispensary came online
Fire department to complete training on new cardiac monitor and phase out LifePak 15s
Trustees and staff to fly to Florida in 4-5 weeks for final Quint inspection
Service department to proceed with outright purchase of dump truck (no financing) and explore F-600 smaller truck option (contact Larry at Harlan Township)
key_amounts: $245,000 – back-pay cannabis dispensary host community funds (July 2024 to present)
$463,000 – 2026 estimated dispensary fund total (including back pay)
$150,000 – budgeted for road paving ($75,000 general fund + $75,000 road and bridge)
$150,000 – service truck/dump truck purchase (outright, no financing)
$300 – food gift card donation from CPAA
$6.4 million – estimated ending unencumbered balance for 2027
$50,000 – safety services levy earmark for motor vehicles
$1.6 million – anticipated general fund ending balance for 2025
~$2 million – projected general fund balance by July 2027
notable_topics: M/I Homes proposed residential development (547-565 lots) on Woodville Pike with two plan options
Trustees' concerns about development not meeting new PD zoning standards post-moratorium
2027 tax budget public hearing and adoption
Cannabis dispensary host community revenue and back-pay distribution
Fire Station 18 ribbon cutting and public open house
New cardiac monitor with live-stream physician capability
Quint fire apparatus nearing completion in Florida
Updated official zoning map adoption (Resolution R022-2020)
Officer Gary Rausch retirement after 47 years
America 250 flag sales and Historical Society newsletter launch
Goshen Township financial health and capital planning
attendees: Lorna Rhodes (Historical Society Vice President, mentioned)
notes: Trustee Hodges was absent; trustees declined to give feedback on M/I Homes plans without all three members present. The board expressed concern about approving developments below the newly adopted PD minimum lot size of 10,800 sq ft immediately after exiting a moratorium. The 2027 tax budget was described as a preliminary planning document required by the county that does not bind the township's actual spending. Steve Pigram humorously referred to the tax budget as the 'BS budget' due to its non-binding nature. The transcript contained significant portions of garbled/unintelligible text likely due to audio issues.
title: BOT 5-26-26
